LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY'S MOTION FOR ORDER ENFORCING TERMS OF SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT. NO APPEARANCE. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY'S MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 664.6 IS DENIED. FORD FAILS TO PRESENT A SETTLEMENT THAT COMPLIES WITH THE DIRECT PARTY PARTICIPATION AND RETAINED JURISDICTION REQUIREMENTS OF 664.6. A CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 998 SETTLEMENT IS NOT THE EQUIVALENT OF A 664.6 SETTLEMENT. IN ADDITION, THE PRESENT DISPUTE IS REALLY BETWEEN PLAINTIFF'S FORMER AND PRESENT COUNSEL, NOT PLAINTIFF AND DEFENDANT. FORD, PLAINTIFF, CALIFORNIA LEMON LAW GROUP ("CLLG"), AND THE KNIGHT LAW GROUP ARE ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER IMMEDIATELY TO RESOLVE THIS ISSUE. IF THEY CANNOT COME TO A RESOLUTION, THEN FORD MAY NEED TO INTERPLEAD THE $25,000 PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 386 AND MAY BE ENTITLED TO RECOVER ITS FEES FOR HAVING TO COMMENCE SUCH AN ACTION FROM THE $25,000. (SEE CITY OF MORGAN HILL V. BROWN (1999) 71 CAL.APP.4TH 1114.) ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN SCHULMAN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Enforcing Terms Of Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Apr-22-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY'S Motion For Order Enforcing Terms Of Settlement Agreement.

Defendant Ford Motor Company's motion to enforce settlement pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure 664.6 is denied. Ford fails to present a settlement that complies with the direct party participation and retained jurisdiction requirements of 664.6. A Code of Civil Procedure 998 settlement is not the equivalent of a 664.6 settlement. In addition, the present dispute is really between Plaintiff's former and present counsel, not Plaintiff and Defendant. Ford, Plaintiff, California Lemon Law Group ("CLLG"), and the Knight Law Group are ordered to meet and confer immediately to resolve this issue. If they cannot come to a resolution, then Ford may need to interplead the $25,000 pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure 386 and may be entitled to recover its fees for having to commence such an action from the $25,000. (See City of Morgan Hill v. Brown (1999) 71 Cal.App.4th 1114.)

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan Schulman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/EPS)
